message:
fix(tls): implement half-close semantics for TlsStream

TlsStream previously moved to Closed state immediately after flushing
close_notify, which prematurely killed the read side. The fix:

- Add read_closed flag tracking peer EOF independently of write shutdown
- ShuttingDown state now only transitions to Closed when read_closed is
  also true (i.e., peer has sent its own close_notify or the transport
  reports EOF)
- note_read_eof() helper centralizes the read-side closure logic
- poll_shutdown no longer sets Closed unless the read side is already done

This implements proper TLS half-close: local shutdown sends close_notify
but the connection remains readable until the peer reciprocates.

Tests updated:
- Existing close_notify test now verifies half-close (client.is_closed()
  is false after shutdown, true after reading peer's close_notify)
- New test: local_shutdown_preserves_read_side_until_peer_eof verifies
  data can still be read after local shutdown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
